message:
fix(web): Enhance SQLite validation error messages with diagnostic info

Improve error reporting when downloaded database fails SQLite header
validation to aid debugging of corrupted downloads or server errors.

Changes:
- Log detailed diagnostic info to console.error before throwing:
  - Total byte length of received data
  - First 32 bytes as hex dump for binary inspection
  - First 64 bytes decoded as ASCII (non-printable chars replaced with .)
- Include received byte count and header preview in error message
- Helps distinguish between: truncated downloads, HTML error pages
  returned instead of database, corrupted transfers, wrong file served

Example error output:
  "Downloaded data is not a valid SQLite database (received 1234 bytes,
   header: "<!DOCTYPE html>..."). The file may be corrupted..."

This makes it immediately clear when a server returns an error page
(starting with <!DOCTYPE or <html) vs actual database corruption
(random bytes) vs incomplete download (valid header but wrong size).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
